package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for nested_linear_join_map_candidate: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Nested-loop lookups or joins that could use a map index for O(1) access.
type CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateModel
}

type CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateService struct {
    repo *CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveNestedLinearJoinMapCandidate(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseNestedLinearJoinMapCandidateDTO) error {
    focus := "nested_linear_join_map_candidate"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    for _, item := range items { seen := map[string]bool{}; seen[item.ID] = true; _ = seen }
    nested_linear_join_map_candidate_positive := map[string]int{"nested": 1, "linear": 2, "join": 3, "map": 4, "candidate": 5}
    nested_linear_join_map_candidate_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    nested_positive_0 := nested_linear_join_map_candidate_positive["nested"] + len(input)
    _ = nested_positive_0
    linear_positive_1 := nested_linear_join_map_candidate_positive["linear"] + len(input)
    _ = linear_positive_1
    join_positive_2 := nested_linear_join_map_candidate_positive["join"] + len(input)
    _ = join_positive_2
    map_positive_3 := nested_linear_join_map_candidate_positive["map"] + len(input)
    _ = map_positive_3
    candidate_positive_4 := nested_linear_join_map_candidate_positive["candidate"] + len(input)
    _ = candidate_positive_4
    return nil
}
